what_it_does: This file is a terrain ledger entry documenting a completed overnight conversation-mining pipeline that read Tim's Claude Code chat history, embedded and clustered the messages, and synthesized a Windows-side Obsidian vault called Conversation-Mind.
observations: The folder name 'mcp-mining' is a misnomer — the orchestrator explicitly rejects MCP framing and predefined categories.
The pipeline uses one resident vLLM model per phase to stay within VRAM after a 35B OOM failure.
Local models are Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit and BAAI/bge-m3; heavier interpretive steps use deepseek-v4-pro via cloud.
The run is dormant/finished: status.json shows phase 'enrich-done' and run.log reports 151 subtopics and 5 deep-dives.
The 59M working directory holds messages, embeddings, clusters, and logs; the deliverable vault lives on the Windows side.
Only 5 of 17 files were read in detail; large data files were sized but not parsed.
It links to corpora, wizard-run-1, and company-scan as related terrain entries.
standouts: The orchestrator docstring explicitly says 'NO predefined categories/questions. NO MCP framing.' despite the entry name.
One-model-per-phase scheduling was introduced as a deliberate fix after a 35B model OOM.
Interpretive heavy lifting is offloaded to a cloud model while local inference handles bulk reading and embedding.
The output artifact is stored outside the project path, on a Windows Obsidian vault.
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry frontmatter schema with type, register, status, coverage, and relation fields.
Uses wiki-style backlinks like [[corpora]] and [[wizard-run-1]] for cross-entry relationships.
Includes evidence-based claims with specific filenames, sizes, and log quotes.
Adds a coverage caveat to limit over-interpretation.
Maintains a neutral, descriptive register without ranking.
concerns: Status is 'unconfirmed' even though run_status is reported finished.
Classification rests on only 5 of 17 files plus file sizes; the enrichment scripts and output vault were not inspected line-by-line.
The name 'mcp-mining' conflicts with the stated content and could mislead retrieval.
No git remote; provenance of the working files beyond the run log is not established.
notes: A dormant, finished chat-history mining run whose ledger entry is careful to say it is not actually about MCP and to flag its own limited file coverage.
questions: What do the 151 subtopics and 5 deep-dives contain, and how are they organized in the vault?
What is the exact structure and quality of the produced Conversation-Mind vault?
How do enrich.py, enrich2.py, and regen_dialectics.py differ in function?
Why is the entry status 'unconfirmed' if the run log reports completion?
What was the original 'MCP' launch context that produced the misnomer?
Were any outputs manually validated against source messages?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to catalog a conversation-mining pipeline; the content matches that, except the 'mcp-mining' label mismatches the actual non-MCP subject.
apparent_themes: Bottom-up emergent synthesis without predefined categories
Resource-constrained local inference with strict one-model-per-phase VRAM discipline
Hybrid local/cloud model loadout for cost and capacity management
Provenance-aware documentation with verbatim quoting intent
Neutral terrain cataloging with explicit coverage limits
intention_signals: The entry seems to be trying to record and make discoverable a one-off mining run, explain its design choices and resource constraints, and honestly situate it among sibling prototypes while warning readers where evidence is thin.
novelty: It documents a personal chat-history corpus being turned into an emergent Obsidian idea-map by a self-directing orchestrator that swaps models to fit GPU memory, while openly rejecting the MCP framing implied by its own name.
contribution: Preserves a concrete worked example of a local-4B + embedding + clustering pipeline applied to conversation history, including the one-model-per-phase VRAM scheduling pattern and a cloud/local split for interpretive work; also preserves its explicit coverage caveat and links to related terrain entries.
summary_for_embedding: A terrain ledger entry for mcp-mining, a completed overnight pipeline that extracted Tim's Claude Code chat history, embedded and clustered it with local Qwen3.5-4B and bge-m3, enriched interpretations via deepseek-v4-pro cloud, and produced a Windows-side Obsidian Conversation-Mind vault.
intention_for_embedding: To document and catalog a finished bottom-up conversation-mining run, its model-loadout discipline, and its relationship to sibling prototypes.
